Hmm, I get this perspective.

And I do like it

In sickness, we change a lot.

I think back to being 17 and carefree. And then my issues with anxiety started kicking off at 18, and the decline and deterioration ensued from there.

Through the many, many years of suffering, I think we can get quite wounded, traumatised, and in my case, suppress a lot of emotion, and lose our personality, sense of self, sense of hope and optimism.

The many distortions of the low energy brain.

I am a true believer in Dr Jack. NOTHING prior to this, worked for me. I have been chasing these ideas, for years, and frankly, they have enabled me to get where I am right now, from being in hell itself, to improving myself, travelling, and running a business.

Why do I share the above?

I do not believe that the current version of myself, is "me", so to speak.

I have healed, but I think I am only 20% of the way there.

I still have a LOT of work to do on my biology. My body and brain is ready to heal, and the work I've done for years on myself, has led me to here.

I can still experience some anxiety, some quite negative and jaded thinking, and the recent ADHD type symptoms, speak volumes on the state of my brain chemistry, mitochondria, and the road I have ahead of me.

My intuition tells me, if I just continue to focus on ME, healing, and really regenerating my biology, I will be able to figure the other things out in my life.
**
Order of Importance**

1\. Restoring clarity and brain function: beating ADHD type symptoms and returning to good thinking ability
2\. Increasing physical energy levels, enabling me to put more vigour and vitality into life
3\. Aggressively grow my business, to get sufficient location independent income to travel further and get stronger light exposure. I am in Central Europe now, which beats the UK, but has not proven to be enough.........
4\. Resume my goals in my personal life, of finding a relationship, building a family

Thanks for listening

\-Ravi

### Ravi Sandhu — 2023-08-23T11:03:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-325460>

**WED 22nd Aug**

Today, I woke up feeling clearer, calmer, and like yesterday, a little better. My mind didn't feel like distractable sludge, and I felt less negative/toxic anger.

I also feel lightly less anxious, worried about the future, and in a better "space".

Energy levels, were OK in the morning, and then crashed.

Is it any wonder my system isn't working so great. My overall energy, isn't good, at all. At best, on the best of days, it is about a 4/10.

My mitochondria, are not shit like they once were. I can wander around in the sun here, when it's 30/32C, and not be bothered by it at all. Not even sweat much! Whilst others are quite stressed by the heat.

This is a sign of improvement of course.

Today's action plan:
\-45m earthing in full spectrum sunlight
\-10m cold thermogenesis (tub is 11C today)
\-Work, with my windows fully open, so getting a lot of light into my apartment
\-Fast until 2pm. Eat chicken, some salmon, some cheese. That's it.
\-Drink lots of RO water.
\-Get to bed at around 10/1030pm.

I am not exercising right now. I am going to heal more and regenerate. Last few weeks, I had really bad performance in the gym. I undertood, it was time to go back to basics and address my healing more.

I am optimistic about the dietary protocol I am using right now. Lots of meat. Some diary (cheese, some milk in coffee). Some fish.

Will report back on the journal tomorrow.

Ravi

Light - 4hrs or so
Keto - DONE

Lets see what tomorrow brings.

Ravi

### Ravi Sandhu — 2023-08-25T08:53:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-325559>

**Friday 25th August**

Woke up, could tell something was off mentally.

Watched the sunrise.

Did CT.

Sat down and worked for a while. Got quite a lot done, though was a bit distracted.

I noticed something about my mood and mental state....

I feel low motivation and low drive today.

And a certain emptiness.

I feel like just laying in bed and doing nothing all day. But I am against that behavior and believe in persistence.

As I reflect, it feels very much like feelings of depression I had in my early 20s.

Is keto depression a thing??

I feel depressed in mood and spirit right now. I can tell. This is quite unlike me.

I will attribute this, to my body adapting to keto again. I mean, the last time I keto adapted, I honestly didn't experience something like this. This is odd.

It's 8am. I'm going to go out in the light and read until 10. Let's see how my day goes.

Any thoughts on why I may be experiencing some type of depressed mood? I am not completely numb or blunted, I can feel a little anger here and there. Odd experience.

\-Ravi

I agree with [@jules3791](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29114/) statement above. I'd also suggest you stop symptom-scanning. Every time you do, you're kicking your limbic system back online and then subsequently releasing the cascade of stress chemicals. Try to pattern interrupt these thoughts/behaviors. Focus on the good, what is going right, the amazingness of the body, etc. You have the tools and hacks in your toolbelt. Incorporate some of the more parasympathetic engaging modalities like breathwork, meditation, CT. Trigger the vagus nerve. And lastly, if your body is telling you to rest, I'm inclined to listen to it and rest.

Still working. Healed myself a lot.

Physically and emotionally a lot healtier now.

Now where I want to be, though. Going to work hard, save money, and go to Mexico for 90days Q3 and heal more.

What I look like now, is attached.

A damn sight better than I was.

32, 33 in July.

Will remain focused.

To get what you seek, it seems like health is just non-negotiable. Though I have healed, a lot, I am not where I want to be in energy, brain function, clarity, and overall consciousness......

Back in the UK now, after 12 months in Budapest. Weather is shit here. I will use my lights, daily. And lots of earthing on the weekend.

Haven't done CT for a few weeks, my gear, such as the Lumi tub and the freezer I used to freeze 100lbs of ice each day, was in Budapest.

I definitely healed a lot. But I am not there yet.............WIll return to posting and thinking more deeply.

\-R

**Looking good**,
but
drink only plain water.
Plain water with CO2 is also ok (may even be better).

Even if what you have is orange juice, it is not ok to drink it.
But it is ok to eat oranges (or other fruits).
In this case it would be about how quickly glucose you are taking, how quickly it raises your insulin and blood glucose.
Slower is desirable,
fast raise is not ok.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-18T11:51:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-332622>

Many thanks for your posts guys.

Yes I do indeed look far better than I used to. This is the outcome of 3 years of consistent work. While I have not been the most regular of posters, I have lived this biohacking lifestyle for 3 years, without any breaks or deviation from this path.

Jan, I was once seduced by Ray Peat's ideas, and was quite bought into this idea of torpor, excess serotonin, and the shut down effects thereof. Frankly, following the ideas of that space, to supposedly snap out of torpor, or whatever this clan purports, got me sicker, more tired, and created the obese mess you see above. Horrible.

Question for you guys...........

On weekends, 3 or 4 times the past 3 months, I have smoked some really good weed. This is something I seldom do, and just tried because my housemate was doing it. I did notice, it was remarkably healing for me, and I felt far more happy, optimistic, and upbeat about life for days afterwards. I felt less stressed, and more integrated and whole. Far less broken, so to speak. I am going to experiment with doing this, on the weekends. It does feel a bit off, to be trying this, at 32, a productive and focused entrepreneur. And yet, mostly in my life, I am not feeling happy or satisfied, and feel quite mired in lack and dislocation.

And yet, for whatever reason, I feel noticeably more whole for days after smoking. What comes to me, when I think this through, is perhaps the weed has an anti-depressant effect on me, and I may very well be reeling off of depression and some long-standing trauma? Stuck in some chronic response of sorts.

Thoughts would be welcome

Re-introduced carbs, and strength in the gym immediately began rocketing up, and I am hitting new personal bests every week. I've been doing well for the last 7 weeks of bringing carbs back in, and have no plans to avoid carbs ever again. I am gaining muscle size, and this is positive.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-19T09:41:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-332650>

Interesting discussion Jan and I do appreciate it.

I can tell you, from going low carb for 2 years, I was barely about to deadlift 100kg, my bench press was around a measly 85kg for the entire duration, and I would never progress in my physique or strength!

Re introducing carbs, and increasing my protein, it was like I'd been injected with steroids.

In 7 weeks, my deadlift went from 100kg to 170kg, that's almost 400lbs, and bench press rocketed up to 105kg.

The only change, was diet.

So, low carb.............?

I did need it, for years, to no longer be obese and very sick. I am glad I healed that stage.

But I am no longer there. I want to be healthy and vibrant. And this is the time in my journey I am exploring new ideas and moving away from low carb.

I will maintain, I did need low carb to survive, and come back into life and the world again. I was dead, more or less. Now, I am alive. But only just...........My brain isn't working like it did when I was 18 and I just need to re-ignite my genius again.

Yesterday:
\-Sunrise, Sunset
\-2hrs earthing
\-2hrs red light therapy (I use the inferno panel)
\-15m sperti red light

Felt rested, recovered, and better this morn.

Will repeat this process and see how I get on. This is the best I can do in England, to be honest.

\-R

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-19T12:52:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-332654>

>

Yup. It's not. But, alas, it's part of my overall process and grand scheme.

Stage 1:
\-Get to 5,000 p/m location independent income (I am just over half way now)
\-Escape to Mexico for 3 months, heal lots, and keep it moving from there

With that being said, with determination and tenacity, I am able to make some ideas work here. I watch sunrise, sunset, work on my laptop in the garden, barefoot, for a few hours a day (yes, the drizzle weather does bring wind, spittles of rain, and the generally unpleasant, muggy atmosphere you'd expect in Britain), perform CT in my tub, and I have some powerful light panels (EMR-Tek Inferno, Sperti Fiji Sun) and believe I am making some progress.

In fact, I will say, when I am consistent, I absolutely do make progress. And improvements in my organism, are observable. It's just, I am damn well aware that this isn't optimal, isn't thriving health and function, is a damn far cry from what constitutes real human vigour and vitality.

More than happy to keep up, let's stay in touch - I am active on Facebook:

### [Log into Facebook](https://www.facebook.com/IronWillTribe/)

Log into Facebook to start sharing and connecting with your friends, family, and people you know.

 www.facebook.com

I'll be here for 2 more months, I expect, and then off elsewhere. And I will be doing a trip to the tropics for 3 months later this year,

Mediocrity just isn't tenable for me.

\-Ravi

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-21T08:33:18+0200

I feel better every day I do this protocol.

2hrs of earthing. Sunrise, Sunset. Red light therapy (Emr Tek Inferno) + the Sperti FiJi sun for 15 m, and I feel better each day.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-25T17:51:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-332821>

It's time to ask for some help.

I need to get out.

I need to find somewhere cheap, in an ideal location for healing, where I can just work remotely on my biz, and work on healing my inner being.

To cut a long story short, I am a childhood trauma survivor, and as I have healed myself more and more, I am now strong enough as a man, and ready to talk about my childhood with a therapist, and begin to heal.

I had an abusive father, who began beating both me and my mother, from the time I was 4 years old. Any type of sordid evil you can imagine, any type of vile abuse of helpless beings, I have seen. Too sick and twisted for words.

And yet, I decided to be a loving, positive, good citizen, who is a supporter and lover of the human animal. Because I always had the unconditional love of my mother, I survived.

I have been doing this work on myself, for 3 years, and now I have started to feel better, I am ready to take on this demon, and bury it with my own hands.

Must get out of here, get some good therapy, spend a lot of time in the sun and in nature, and truly, truly heal.

I will be the one who breaks this cycle, and creates a happy family of unconditional love, nurture, and principled right living.

This madness has gone on for generations. It ends with me.

\-Ravi

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-03-25T21:16:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-332833>

> [JanSz said:](/goto/post?id=332828)
>

> (Make sure that professional psychologist is not reading this, since he already sees new Porsha on his driveway (paid by you).
> Please rethink your situation, and research about human behavior years back.

> From continuation of family life, it may not be the most optimal.
>
> I do not know about your neighborhood.
> But I will tell you about mine. (from pov of man).
> If man does not beat his wife, means that he does not love her.
> Children are coerced to correct behavior, as defined by their parents, by beating them from time to time.
>
> ..

Jan it is curious to me that you make this point.

Clearly, you have underlying suspicion and some toxic beliefs yourself. This post, is a defense mechanism from you. Quite telling. Elsewhere, the ladies on this thread, did highlight that you may have some attitudes and ideas about relationships that may be worth challenging and exploring.

My experience is, that women are really good at picking up on certain things, are generally given far more emotional and social intelligence than men are, and the good ones out there, are really f\*\*king awesome. To meet those good ones, you have to fix yourself. I am currently quite broken, so I have attracted a lot of broken. I blame myself, not the girls, and see some of the situations I've ended up in as useful feedback on how I can improve as a human.

Now, my dear Jan, I must stress, whilst there may be some instances where the father isn't necessarily abusive, in mine, let me assure you, it was one of the truly savage cases. My father, was genuinely a demon, who beat me and my mother, didn't speak to me or acknowledge me until I was 27, and on my 16th birthday, came up to me in the morning of and told me he hated my guts. He was pretty much a miserable, vile fuck, from the get go, and over the decades, revealed just how much of a dysfunctional and f\*\*ked up person he truly was.

A real piece of work. I've not, to this day, had a proper conversation with this person, and I lived with my parents for decades. That is not inept, full of rage, resentment, spite, and embitterment that guy was.

I have written the full story elsewhere, and those who read it, understand how truly brutal it was, and supported my decision to pursue therapy and healing.

Your response is quite interesting. You perhaps had a decent male role model, so have no concept of this. I had a completely evil, demon of a father, he brought chaos and destruction and ruined a few lives. Me, my brother, and my Mum, despite the sadism inflicted, decided we will not be a statistic.

So we've been healing. And we are going to keep thriving and will flourish.

Regarding child rearing and wanting a family - I personally, want to live in a healthy happy family structure. I never got one, and I want to give the next generation one. Yes, finding a partner today, is tough. I have tried, and did about 200 dates in the end, and didn't find anyone I would want to be with long term. The answer, is in healing oneself further, exploring other parts of the world, and just continuing to be open, and keeping a positive mental attitude.

If we are open to the possibility, and have the right ideas and thinking, as well as truly good intention, then we can find answers, attract good things, and make good things happen.

Cynicism, questioning what the point even is, and having a jaded mindset, is all too easy today, given the challenging climate. Giving into this tendency, does not tend to support winning in any endeavour in life. They're just excuses. In this journey, I am committed to making none.

\-Ravi

This is not OK. EVER. Not in any context, at any time, EVER.

You live today.
Be happy and be gentle.
What you think of you mom and dad, they may care about, or may not.
They may have loved you or your existence might have messed up their life.

My self, from grade 1 to 4 I was a teacher's pet.
The whole four grade school was in one room, one teacher. Some children were rather old. The war barely ended; the older children still had guns that they were using in war. I was the youngest there. The teacher who was trying to arrange for school existence, worked with a limit and was still missing one child.
So, he talked my parents to send me a year earlier.
May people had PTSD from war experiences.
Grades from 5-7 were in nearby town.
Teachers there were having sticks with which they punished unruly students.
Some who were really bad were sent home so their parents could punish them.

/////
Beating was part of living.

..

I respect Jan and his views, Jan has been a consistently very supportive member, has given me a lot of helpful and supportive advice, and many of the things I learned from him, helped me heal a lot as a person. So I am grateful to [@JanSz](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/933/)

I saw that, because I was once there, very wounded, and I blamed my mother for bringing my abusive father into my life, and I stored a lot of anger, tension, and mistrust of women. What I learned as a small boy, is that when you love a woman (mother), she will bring a lot of chaos into your life, so it's best to just detach.

As a man, who has worked on himself and healed over 3 years, I can now see, that this is a very wounded way of thinking and was/is my trauma.

The better pathway forward, is literally to find ways to just love. Fully, deeply, and heal yourself, and others, by giving the world the self actualised version of you.

So community..........

[@Ravi Sandhu](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/25364/)
It is probably good time to end this story.
I wish you luck in your pursuits for better life.
Overall, it is likely that it may be better to look at life as a number of periods, rather than one, especially when considering tying up with someone in effort to procreate.

I am mostly thinking about marriage.
If you think of marital vows, there is a line there "till death do us part"
If marriage is supposed to last 40, 50 or more years,
there is a little chance that everyday both of you will be in agreement on everything, during marriage.

So instead of getting married think of indefinite agreement to live together with someone that you like today, until one of you wants to split.

There is no sense to make agreement for life, when you know today that the is a small chance that you will be able to hold on to your vows.

My self, I just was lucky, it has nothing to do with me being smart or not.
That is why, number of times I posted that:
I rather be lucky than smart.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2024-05-27T18:04:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-335190>

Doing a lot of healing & emotional work

Lots of time in the sun.

Feeling better and stronger. But not optimal yet.

Will keep working, increasing my income, and getting healthier.

Relationships are complex in 2024. The gal I am seeing went on a trip for 3 weeks, and despite her saying she doesn't want to date other people, immediately got back on the online dating apps and switched locations to where she is based for the time being. lul. Oh well. No worries. Will keep on keeping on as per usual and just keep chatting normally. If she has any semblance of interest when she gets back, I can just see what the deal is, or have "the talk" and move on.

These little tells of low integrity behaviour just aren't my preference. We as men, notice a lot. Especially if you've been in this arena for as long as I have.

Generally, there are some women of real substance and value, who have a healthy sense of boundaries and know how to build towards a healthy monogomous relationship. I call these Unicorns, and to find one, you have to be stoic and just grind through so, so many (been on dates with 200+ atm)

I'm not sweating it. Little things like this, and other low integrity behaviours (saying you are teetotal, and then drinking...saying you don't do drugs...and smoking weed) show one's inner world. And we as men do pick it all up. We know in our core when someone just isn't right. The unconscious makes a decision to "move on" before the outward behaviours align with that and we express our truth clearly and verbally.

Internally, I've moved on from this one. I'll continue speaking to her daily, and she has left her stuff at my place, so when she returns from her trip, I'll let her take her things and continue to be very positive.

But these sort of things just aren't my preference and given that I myself am not seeing other people, I kinda wanted the same energy.

No worries. I will keep growing and find a better person.

I am not mad and I do not mind the large trial and error involved.

\-Ravi

### JanSz — 2024-07-02T18:49:02+0300

**Plenary Lecture - The Neurobiology of Long-Term Memory - Cristina Maria Alberini**

### JanSz — 2024-07-02T18:51:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-336455>

\======================

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/post-325136](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/long-covid-higher-brain-heteroplasmy-due-to-melanopsin-damage.27749/post-325136)

neurodegenerative diseases = Alzheimer’s disease, Parkinson’s disease, FTD, Huntington’s disease.

\=====================================================================

Alzheimer’s disease is characterized by the abnormal build-up of proteins in and around brain cells1.

There are two key abnormal protein deposits that accumulate in the brain during the development of Alzheimer’s disease2:

Amyloid-beta protein: Deposits of this protein form plaques around brain cells1.

These sticky, abnormal deposits disrupt brain cells and their connections to each other, and eventually, the brain cells die3.

Tau protein: Deposits of this protein form tangles within brain cells1.

These deposits disrupt communication among neurons, resulting in loss of function and cell death4.

Detecting the buildup of these proteins at an early stage may allow clinicians to intervene before the condition has a chance to take hold2.

\=================================================================

There are some other diseases that cause abnormal protein deposits in the brain:

1.  **Lewy Body Dementia (LBD)**: This is a progressive dementia that results from protein deposits in nerve cells of the brain. [It affects movement, thinking skills, mood, memory, and behavior](https://www.msn.com/en-us/health/condition/Lewy-body-dementia/hp-Lewy-body-dementia?source=conditioncdx)[1](https://www.msn.com/en-us/health/condition/Lewy-body-dementia/hp-Lewy-body-dementia?source=conditioncdx). [The proteins, called Lewy bodies, mainly affect areas involved in thinking, memory, and movement](https://www.msn.com/en-us/health/condition/Lewy-body-dementia/hp-Lewy-body-dementia?source=conditioncdx)[1](https://www.msn.com/en-us/health/condition/Lewy-body-dementia/hp-Lewy-body-dementia?source=conditioncdx).
2.  **Parkinson’s Disease**: This is a neurodegenerative disorder that leads to progressive deterioration of motor function due to the loss of dopamine-producing brain cells. [Similar to LBD, Parkinson’s disease is associated with the accumulation of alpha-synuclein protein in the brain](https://www.ninds.nih.gov/health-information/disorders/lewy-body-dementia)[2](https://www.ninds.nih.gov/health-information/disorders/lewy-body-dementia).
3.  **Multiple System Atrophy (MSA)**: This is a rare neurodegenerative disorder characterized by autonomic dysfunction, parkinsonism, and ataxia. [It is associated with the deposition of a type of alpha-synuclein protein known as Papp-Lantos bodies in the brain](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/04/200429201706.htm)[3](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2020/04/200429201706.htm).
4.  **Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS)**: Also known as Lou Gehrig’s disease, ALS is a specific disorder that involves the death of neurons controlling voluntary muscles. [It is one of many types of neurodegenerative disorders that are due to the accumulation of abnormal proteins in the brain](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/22934-brain-diseases)[4](https://my.clevelandclinic.org/health/diseases/22934-brain-diseases).

\=================================================================

[Insulin-like growth factors 1 and 2 **(IGF-1 and IGF-2)** play a key role in the maintenance of the nervous tissue viability](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079).

[They exhibit neuroprotective effects by stimulating migration and proliferation of nervous cells, activating cellular metabolism, inducing regeneration of damaged cells, and regulating various stages of prenatal and postnatal development of the nervous system](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079).

[The availability of IGFs for the cells is controlled via their interaction with the IGF-binding proteins (IGFBPs) that inhibit their activity](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079).

[On the contrary, the cleavage of IGFBPs by specific proteases leads to the IGF release and activation of its cellular effects](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079).

[Therefore, the release of IGF by proteolytic cleavage of IGFBPs is an important mechanism of neuronal protection](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079)[1](https://link.springer.com/article/10.1134/S0006297923140079).

\==================================================================

[**Cristina Maria Alberini is a Professor in Neuroscience at the Center for Neural Science in New York University**, and an associate Investigator at the Neuroscience Institute NYU Langone Medical Center, NY](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini).

[She is known for her research on the biological mechanisms of long-term memory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini).

[Her research focuses on understanding the cellular and molecular mechanisms underlying the stabilization, storage, and consolidation of long-term memories](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini).

[Another part of her research involves the study of memory retrieval and reconsolidation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini)[1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cristina_Alberini).

[In her lecture **“The Neurobiology of Long-Term Memory: Key Molecules, Diverse Cell Types, Temporal Dynamics, and Critical Periods”**, she presents major findings on key biological mechanisms regulated in response to learning, their expression in diverse cell types, their temporal dynamics, and their roles in long-term memory formation as well as changes induced by memory recall](https://www.sissa.it/it/calendar-event/plenary-lecture-neurobiology-longterm-memory-key-molecules-diverse-cell-types)[2](https://www.sissa.it/it/calendar-event/plenary-lecture-neurobiology-longterm-memory-key-molecules-diverse-cell-types).

**Plenary Lecture - The Neurobiology of Long-Term Memory - Cristina Maria Alberini**

\=================================================================

[**Mind Over Molecules: The Biology of Memory**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YYZnoe4ZNv4)

[World Science Festival](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YYZnoe4ZNv4)

[29K views **7 days ago**](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=YYZnoe4ZNv4)

////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

[Insulin‐like growth factor‐2 is a promising candidate for the treatment and prevention of Alzheimer's disease - Fitzgerald - 2023 - CNS Neuroscience & Therapeutics - Wiley Online Library](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/cns.14160)

[https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/cns.14160](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1111/cns.14160)

**Insulin-like growth factor-2 is a promising candidate for the treatment and prevention of Alzheimer's disease**

[G. S. Fitzgerald](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/authored-by/Fitzgerald/G.+S.), [T. G. Chuchta](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/authored-by/Chuchta/T.+G.), [E. C. McNay](https://onlinelibrary.wiley.com/authored-by/McNay/E.+C.)

First published: 27 March 2023

### Anthony Smith — 2024-10-27T20:38:35+0200

What I have noticed with many clients over the years is that some (EMF mitigation) techniques help people that are very sick get less sick BUT these same techniques do not help “less sick” get well. They can even make people who are basically “well” feel worse. Less nnEMF is alway better BUT the best way to go about it can change. What works for one may not work for another depending where you are on the journey.

For example . . . I find it interesting that the same people who tell us to get rid of metal bed frames and metal coils (sometimes to buy their $10,000 cotton and organic foam beds, which may be VERY good by themselves) are ok with metallic grounding sheets or pads which are then connected by wire to the house electrical system or a 50ft antenna. This may help very sick people get less sick but we have MANY reports of “well” people having bad results with in home grounding. Ground outside in Nature with your body in direct contact with the Earth.

I share this because there may be people here on the Forum that are grounding themselves inside and still having problems. Ground appliances NOT you. Grounding inside make you part of the circuit and the 60Hz (50hz) AC fields now have a path of least resistance - through YOU - to ground. At night TURN OFF the AC circuits in your bedroom, get up in the morning and watch the sun rise with your body directly in contact with the Earth . . . It is called “EARTHING” for a reason.

I'm currently doing the EMT-Tek lights for 30 minutes a day, though I don't always do it twice; perhaps I should try 15 minutes in the morning and 15 in the evening. I also try to see the sunrise each morning as much as I can - I live facing east and have a balcony on the Hudson River so I can see the Sun rise and often sit out and greet it. As Jack mentioned in his latest blog post (Jab Part 2), I’m not vaccinated (thank goodness!), but I'm committed to living a healthy lifestyle that works for me, given my location. There is some leeway in that, especially for those who are unvaccinated.

What I gathered from the recent blog post is that for those who are vaccinated, there is NO leeway. You must see the sunrise every day, get as much sun exposure as possible, and possibly even relocate closer to the equator, potentially on a permanent basis. Additionally, it's important to get a lot of red light and near-infrared (NIR) exposure, as this could reduce the risk of developing aggressive cancers. Certainly, the jabbed should have red-light lamps of good quality available to use daily. I also got a Sperti lamp that I am using every other day.

I do not understand the biochemistry and quantum concepts as well as I should Jack discusses, and I still find it challenging to fully grasp that part of the conversation. He indicates that those who are vaccinated might to become experts in this area. I'm more or less following the Leptin RX, although I may not be as strict as I could be.

Ravi, the person you mentioned might be the original individual I was responding to. I'm not entirely sure due to the way the thread is structured; it can be unclear who is responding to whom. Nevertheless, I think I understand the general points being made.

One question that arose for me from Jack's latest blog post is, "Why can we function normally with 40% non-defective mitochondria?" Is that simply a result of normal aging, perhaps combined with lifestyle choices and habits that are essential for our health and our ability to create structured water in coherent domains?

-   **10:00–11:00** **Admin Hour** (timer on; stop at 60:00)
-   **11:00–12:00** **Life Wisdom Lab** (Arthur C. Brooks + 1 GGIA practice)
    -   Mission (1 line): \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
    -   One boundary (this week): \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
-   **12:00–12:50** **Training / Mobility** (4 moves or mobility flow)
-   **1:00–2:30** **YouTube Sales Engine — Inbound Study (EasyGrow)**
    -   Channel promise locked? ☐
    -   3-video funnel outlined? ☐
    -   Upload defaults (offer link top) updated? ☐
-   **2:00–3:00** **Play: Padel** (light, fun)
-   **3:30** See **Carl?** ☐ Yes ☐ No → If yes, time/place: \_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_\_
-   **Recovery** Sauna & **Sunset** (3 “awe reps”)
-   **Guilt-free TV** (watch absolute bullshit )
-   **Bed** (lights low, quick stretch)

* * *

### Essentials (each ✓ = 5 pts, aim 8–10)​

\[ \] Outside light ≤60 min of waking
\[ \] 30+ min movement (walk/gym/mobility)
\[ \] Admin hour started & **stopped** at 60
\[ \] DND on during joy/study blocks
\[ \] One social touchpoint (padel/Carl/call/text)
\[ \] One play/creative act (padel or quick writing)
\[ \] One service/meaning act (cleanup/voice note/donation)
\[ \] 20+ min learning (Brooks/GGIA or EasyGrow)
\[ \] Sunset/awe moment
\[ \] Evening Close done
**Essentials subtotal:** \_\_\_\_ / 50

* * *

### Feel Scores (0–10 each)​

Joy \_\_\_\_ Calm \_\_\_\_ Connection \_\_\_\_ Vitality \_\_\_\_ Novelty/Awe \_\_\_\_
**Feels subtotal:** \_\_\_\_ / 50
**TOTAL:** \_\_\_\_ / **100**

-   90–100 = Bliss Badge
-   75–89 = Solid Weekend
-   <75 = Learning Weekend (pick one lever to fix tomorrow)

* * *

### Ravi Sandhu — 2025-11-14T21:00:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-362654>

Yesterday’s healing session was so powerful man.

How I process, is my subconscious and unconscious, does integration work, and my focus is therefore used up as mental bandwidth goes elsewhere

I felt a tension and painful sensation on my heart side. A symptom of the energetic binds clearing, heart chakra opening.

I woke up at 2am, just wired with cortisol, a cleansing reaction.

I couldnt get back to sleep.

First alarm went off at 5am, I could not move (trauma processing)

Same thing happened at 6am....

72 hours after big trauma clearing sessions, you are deeply restructuring the psyche, and the work is being done "on you". This is the power of skilled therapy, skilled coaching, genuine transformational work with an operator who has a genuine toolkit and knows the art and science of transformation.

Always, invest in this.

Anyway....I'm processing today, so it is what it is:

### Ravi Sandhu — 2025-11-17T15:09:19+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-362730>

I began having an enormous trauma clearing response, about 4pm Friday. I have been offline for the most part, just rolling with it.

It got worse on Saturday, with high fever, big emotions, fight/flight.

A big theme, was understanding, my trauma load is very significant.

About a year in here, I was a ghost really, still working, but becoming progressively more unwell.

I recognised this, about year 3 before my first Mex trip, and knew I was battling something very severe.

Since then, it took over a year of trial and error, to find the approaches that actually work for trauma recovery.

This may just be the rest of the journey. It may be the work for the rest of my life, 10+ years.

Nothing matters more than happiness, self love, and actually truly wanting to be here.

In spirituality, you're ready when you're ready. I also suspect I needed the convergence of biohacking and NDT; the work, floors you. The release of deep, systemic, froze trauma, and the neurological wounds that have been laid down, that mean, the emotional world I live in is very distorted, at times, and makes me engage in self sabotaging decisions. The mechanics of my own system, seems to be, following the weekly 2-3hr trauma clearing session, my body develops a fever, and all energy is used for restructuring work. It seems, the heat in the body, is used to burn off dead, wounded cellular material, contributing to distortions.

I have done the work on deciding what I want, and it is self love, happiness, and inner peace.

Removing these wounds.

Raising my freqeuncy, building heart coherence, building a positive mental attitude.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2025-12-08T13:30:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-363689>

I have continued to do the hard work on myself, and have been doing intensive weekly therapy, forgiveness work, and making new decisions. I am still in Cabo. I returned to the UK, briefly, and then went to Poland, where I crashed. I came back to Cabo, and have slowly been improving. I am eating carbs, as I am back in training, so my mind is not as sharp as it would be in ketosis. But, I get pleasure and joy from training, so will continue.

I am working on changing myself and how I see, relate, behave. I recognised, I had gotten into a cycle of superficial and casual relationships. For context, I was sick until I was 30, and had been agorophobic and had not spoken to other humans for years at a time, let alone go on a date. I had no experience at all with women, and at 30, just confronted it the only way I know how: hard work and determination. Went on 200+ dates over the last few years, and whilst the first year and a half of that was mostly a mess of soft rejection, eventually, I changed, and adapted into a man who could get into casual trysts and sexual encounters. After dozens of those, I began to experience negative emotions post sex & recognised, it had fucked me up. I then stopped, and went through a cycle of therapists, celibacy, abstinance, and working on what the hell went so wrong with me.

After a lot of inner work on myself, I am attracting better now.

I am talking to a nice girl, and progressing, differently from before. Mostly, we relate, as friends. We will feel each other out & take it as it comes. No expectation, other than to see if there is compatability for life partnership. Which is what I seek, and indeed, always did. The decisions I made before, were out of the pain of having lived a life of chronic sickness, chronic anxiety, isolation, loneliness, and suffering.

I believe I am on a better track relationally, and I will seek to deepen this.

What has helped the most, was improving my communication skills, and also, doing the work in men's groups.

\-R

### Ravi Sandhu — 2025-12-08T13:34:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-363690>

I would say, one of my key challenges at the moment, is my battery, isn't doing great. I experience task resistance, most of the time. Lowered dopamine tone. Shifting this, will be needed to get to the next level.

My current protocol for working on this, is based on increasing the ammount of full spectrum light I am getting.

I am in Cabo, and I watch sunrise for 90mins daily, have breakfast, and then I spend until noon absorbing light on the deck.

I then work, go train, do inner work, and so on.

### Ravi Sandhu — 2025-12-08T22:37:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-363708>

Today, I am going to work more intuitively, with less structure, but with more flow, space, and seeking flow states......

I am working on the concept of seeing the brain and nervous system, as a plant - conceiving of our organism, as one which requires light, to heal, repair, and transform!

The transmutation of light photons, into potential energy for transformation!

Today, as I kick off my work day, much of the work I have done on myself in therapy, is sinking into me; into my being, into the formless part of who I am.

I believe, the rigidity of structure, can help us, when we need to activate ourselves.

When we need to align habits systems, and lifestyle.

Mine, is all solid: always up at sunrise, without fail, under any circumstances. Always in bed, devices off, at 8pm. I do not miss.

Between, I heal, I grow, I work.

The key driver for progress, is getting 6 - 8 hours of full spectrum, high UV light here in Cabo San Lucas, to repair my nervous system, find groundedness, and find a deeper peace and focus.

Today, my work will be focused on:

\-Coaching delivery + Checkins
\-TSG Planning: Content Plan + 6 Month Curriculum + Misc
\-Decluttering Tasks: Catching up on various odds and ends

Onwards, with intent towards allowing our inner guidance system, to allow us to manifest Greatness!

\-Ravi

### Ravi Sandhu — 2026-01-08T22:34:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-transformation-ravis-journal-forgiveness-changing-all-my-relationships.25642/post-365008>

Doing far better man

Doing deep forgiveness work, as I have learned from following my teacher online [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/)

I have removed all energy vampires from my life, and people who were keeping me in a dysfunctional pattern of superficial relationships and casual sex

I have a deep inner fear of vulnerability. I developed this, in childhood, as my father was a very abusive man who broke my psychological foundation. However, I have an incredible mother, who is my biggest supporter - this is why I am a good person and a loving, kind man

With deep commitment and charisma, I was able to convince a motivated and driven team, based in NYC, to let me become their CEO & Co-Founder

I will be building a company based on authentic and real relationships, human community, and attacking the singledom epidemic

### [The Single Generation™](https://www.youtube.com/@thesinglegeneration)

Stop freezing up around women. This channel installs calm, confident social skill through a reps-based system: Weekly missions • scripts • real-world drills • accountability frameworks. If you want consistent results, subscribe — and start here: ▶ START HERE (playlist): ⚡ Start Fast: $1...

 www.youtube.com

100% values led, ethical, premium company

My biz partner, is already married and in a supportive, loving relationship with his wife, and is a world class expert

I am the internet marketer behind the operation

I will scale.

I am going to be in Mexico for 3 more months, and then go to NYC to brand build.

I came here, to end the long standing pattern of suffering and pain in my life. I have used the power Dr Jack has helped me find, to heal more, and to end the transgenerational cycle of pain in my family, which has not been resolved

Until a lion like me came along.

It ends, with me. After me, with start healthy, happy families. We embrace nature. We embrace God.

Light. Water. And Magnetism.

Onwards,
\-Ravi

Depending on your background, listening to all this is a (sort of) wasting your time (after) you are convinced that it is forever when you buy bitcoin.
You must arrange your life based on the (potential) you have and not pay for stuff with bitcoins or satoshi.
\-------------
1\. you have x number of Picassos or Rembrant, (or Bitcoins) (they are worth) (X USA $) today, and they will be worth much more 10 years later. The difference is (DeltaX)
2\. you go to the bank, get a 10 years loan whose (total payment + interest) is less than (DeltaX), say (half of DeltaX)
3\. You make your lifestyle based on (Half DeltaX), the bank takes the other (Half DeltaX)
4\. At the end of (good free and healthy) 10 years, you still 100% owe your Picassos or Rembrant (or Bitcoins), go to #2, rinse and repeat.

continuing
The bank should give a mortgage equal to 75% or collateral.
1000000/0.75=13333333
Assume that now you have (Picasso, Rembrandt, or Bitcoin) worth of 1333333 US$ (1.33 mill)
then you can get a 1 mill loan on 4% 30 years long (refinancing, early repayment MUST be allowed)
4774.15\*12=57289.84 yearly payment goes to the bank
your horizon is 10 years, that 1 mill is expected to last you for 10 years, (100k per year)
Good length of the horizon is a must, you must give time to market to figure out its ups and downs
**You can spend 100000-57289.84=42710.16 per year for the first 10 years**
At the end of 10 years, you still owe 787,839.59
Time to look up to your (Picasso, Rembrandt, or Bitcoin) and see how much are they worth now.
The expectation is that we will outrun hyperinflation.
Assuming 25% hyperinflation per year (on average).
Ten years from now, our (Picasso, Rembrandt, or Bitcoin) should be worth
13333333\*1.25^10=124176340.2
that is 124 million
banks should give us a loan equal to 75% of that value
93132255.13
93 million

rinse and repeat
\---------------------------
Note
if your (Picasso, Rembrandt, or Bitcoin) reaches value of 124 million faster, I see no reason to not re-finance sooner, but currently it is an IF.

### Kathleen Morrell — 2021-03-23T23:07:33+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/health-junkie.25648/post-296548>

Hiya!
I am a health nut and very active person. I am always curious about the new and old ways to stay healthy and fit! I discovered Dr. Jack Kruse on Clubhouse and I am so excited about his information he shares (I’m now eating more fish and out in the sun without sunscreen) so that is what brought me here!
I learned a lot about eating healthy and staying active from my parents. It was pretty simple...fresh foods in their purest forms. Backing up a bit...My dad came from Zimbabwe and mom from Cape Town. My father was fortunate to get an engineering scholarship to Stanford and by doing so and lots of travels around the world we were able become US citizens. Growing up my parents always had a garden, chickens and lots of animals. We usually had large properties that as a kid I was always exploring on either with a dog or on a horse. To this day my parents who are now 80 years old and still healthy and somewhat active.
I live in Orange County, California near the beach. I love hiking with my dog, yoga with my friends, stand up Paddle boarding, eating great food and drinking red wine! We buy fresh foods and fish from our local market. I also have a garden myself.
I do not have many health issues but work hard to keep it that way. My health goal is to start studying the info Dr. Jack Kruse has provided and go from there. Thank you, Dr. Jack Kruse!

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-from-a-new-member-moving-to-florida.25650/post-296564>

Hi Everyone!

My wife and I currently live in Seattle, and we've made the decision to sell our house in our overpriced neighborhood and move to Florida because we're in desperate need of sunshine.
We're looking at the north part of Florida, likely the panhandle area. We'll be visiting in April to look at some places.
We've heard that Perry, Lake City, and Milton are good options. Anyone have any opinions about that?
Of course, we'd like to be away from densely populated areas and ideally would like to find a few acres.
If anyone else is going through this process, I'd love to share notes.

Thanks!
Joel

Good luck on your optimal journey to health and wealth!

# Thread: Embrace your inner stoic to understand Bitcoin well.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embrace-your-inner-stoic-to-understand-bitcoin-well.25653/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2021-03-25T15:46:59+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embrace-your-inner-stoic-to-understand-bitcoin-well.25653/post-296613>

“The wise man knows exactly what value should be put upon everything.” — Seneca

The first characteristic of great Stoic leaders is that they are excellent judges of the value (_axia_) of things.

[Zeno of Kition](https://dailystoic.com/zeno/), the founder of the school, had learned this great lesson from [Crates of Thebes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crates_of_Thebes), the Cynic philosopher who was a student of [Diogenes of Sinope](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Diogenes). Diogenes’ father oversaw the minting coins in his native land, and he devalued the currency by reducing the actual content of silver in them. This earned Diogenes and his family an exile that brought him to Athens where Diogenes turned an insight about the true worth of currency into a core philosophical principle—that we should never grant more value to something than it is really worth. [Zeno](https://dailystoic.com/zeno/) built [Stoicism](https://dailystoic.com/what-is-stoicism-a-definition-3-stoic-exercises-to-get-you-started/) on this fundamental idea that the worth of external things is never greater than the moral worth of our own character.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-03-25T15:51:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embrace-your-inner-stoic-to-understand-bitcoin-well.25653/post-296614>

Centuries later [Epictetus](https://dailystoic.com/epictetus/), the former slave turned one of [Stoicism](https://dailystoic.com/what-is-stoicism-a-definition-3-stoic-exercises-to-get-you-started/)’s greatest teachers, would continue to emphasize this core character trait with his students. He taught his students that with every impression we should think of ourselves as an assayer—one who is testing a coin for its actual precious content. The word he used repeatedly for this testing of impressions was _dokimazein_ (to assay, or check the quality of the mineral content of coins). A skilled merchant, he would say, could toss a coin on the table and simply hear the difference between something of real value and a fake—just like a musician immediately spots the sour note in a performance.

That internal assayer, what [Epictetus](https://dailystoic.com/epictetus/) called our ruling reason (_hegemonikon_, the ruling part), was essential for everyone and most especially for [leaders](https://dailystoic.com/leadership/). What we choose to value, how much importance we assign to things, determines where we direct our activity and the results that follow. [Bad leaders](https://dailystoic.com/leadership/#what-mistakes-do-leaders-commonly-make) fail at this most fundamental task, which is why Epictetus put it like this:

_“When it comes to money, where we feel our clear interest, we have an entire art where the tester uses many means to discover the worth…just as we give great attention to judging things that might steer us badly. But when it comes to our own ruling principle, we yawn and doze off, accepting any appearance that flashes by without counting the actual cost.”_

[Great leaders](https://dailystoic.com/leadership/) don’t get worked up by every impression, good or bad, that flashes on their radar. Instead, they take the time and all means necessary to test the actual truth and worth of the matter at hand. Soundness of judgment depends on this.

### JanSz — 2021-03-26T19:58:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/embrace-your-inner-stoic-to-understand-bitcoin-well.25653/post-296663>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=296614)
>
> Centuries later [Epictetus](https://dailystoic.com/epictetus/), the former slave turned one of [Stoicism](https://dailystoic.com/what-is-stoicism-a-definition-3-stoic-exercises-to-get-you-started/)’s greatest teachers, would continue to emphasize this core character trait with his students.

One of these days you may want to discuss the topic of slavery (on no reply thread).
Possibly there is already a You-Tube program like the one you pointed us to on American Finances/Food/Medicine.

Slaves who were able to be philosophers must have had food and shelter and free time to think about their preferred interests.
I am sure there were many (free and hungry) and envious peoples near them.

I think that like most of many other topics slavery it is greatly misdescribed and misevaluated.
Especially when it comes to American former slaves.
Make sure to not overlook places where (outright) slavery was replaced by serfdom.
\--------------------------------------------
The Emancipation Proclamation, or Proclamation 95, was a presidential proclamation and executive order issued by United States President Abraham Lincoln on September 22, 1862, during the Civil War.

**In** the Russian Empire, before the emancipation of the serfs in **1861**, landowners had the right to own serfs to farm their land.
\---------------------------------------------
Those emancipations had more to do with the need for factory labor than pity over slaves.

# Thread: What is one way light transmits information?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-one-way-light-transmits-information.25655/>
**Subforum:** Redox Rx
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2021-03-27T15:08:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-is-one-way-light-transmits-information.25655/post-296679>

How does light carry information?
The twist of light is a key to its ability. The orbital angular momentum of light (OAM) is the component of the angular momentum of a light beam that is dependent on the field spatial distribution, and not on the polarization. ... The internal OAM is an origin-independent angular momentum of a light beam that can be associated with a helical or twisted wavefront.
A thin light beam, such as that emitted by a laser, may possess a hidden rotational structure, invisible to the naked eye. This structure is rooted in the electromagnetic wave nature of light and it takes two distinct forms, which may be dubbed spin and twist. Spin is associated with the rotation of the electric and magnetic fields oscillating within the optical wave—i.e., the circular polarization of light. Twist instead occurs in light waves having a helical-shaped (or twisted) wavefront and an optical vortex located at the beam axis. When a free material particle absorbs light having spin or twist, it is itself made to spin—in other words, the light exerts a rotational form of radiation pressure, showing that this kind of light carries angular momentum. More specifically, the angular momentum of light having circular polarization is named spin angular momentum (SAM), while that associated with a spiral wavefront is called orbital angular momentum (OAM). This is how light communicates with the quantum spin states of electrons and protons. [https://i.stack.imgur.com/UC8Of.png](https://i.stack.imgur.com/UC8Of.png)

From the studies in plasma physics we find -> light's spin creates an optical analogue of a tornado**

Helices spin and counterspin simotaineously.

The image in the above post is a bit "wrong"; in that -> there are two helices -> one moving in one direction and other counter to it -> or what is known in [physics as mirror image](https://www.sciencealert.com/why-do-mirrors-flip-things-horizontally-but-not-vertically-here-s-the-physics) or in [chemistry mirror image](https://chem.libretexts.org/Bookshelves/Organic_Chemistry/Supplemental_Modules_\(Organic_Chemistry\)/Chirality/Chirality_and_Stereoisomers); both use the idea of "mirror image" but have a different application.

Let's start with a simple idea ->

A geometric interpretation of distance in octaves by means of the helix of pitch. (a) The whole number of turns separating the lower limit of pitch f min from the frequency f of the tone. (b) The inclusion of phase in the helix of pitch is such that its effect is to rotate the whole helix by an angle corresponding to its magnitude.

Now let's lay the images over one another ->

The color contrast help "visualize" both helices moving counter spin to each other.

Now let's _try_ to think about this beyond "two dimensions"

And how has _Nature_ shown us? ->

# Thread: A short history up to now.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-short-history-up-to-now.25657/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Josie Thomson — 2021-03-28T17:49:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-short-history-up-to-now.25657/post-296696>

Hello every one, I know you are all healthy as can be on here because of the all the good hacking of light, sleep, redoxing etc. This is such an inspiring place to be a part of, thank you!

I am so grateful to have found Jack who's 'mind blowing' talks on quantum biology really do make my head hurt hahaa!

I feel a bit exposed.. but will do my best to explain as best I can where I am in my Epi Paleo Redox.

Born in Totnes, in the South of England. I had my tonsils and adenoids removed at 8yrs old for allergies. Brought up eating meat, I had migraines from the age of 10, mom wanted to be vegetarian so we went to a naturopath who told me to stop eating dairy, red meat and chocolate, I still got the migraines but from thereon, not knowing what I was doing with no help from the internet, it wasn't around then, I carried on eating a very basic vegetarian diet for most of my life. I live 13 miles from the Exmouth coast, I'm married, 55 post menopausal I've gone through the peri-menopausal/menopause with depression, mood changes, problems with libido. I haven't done any blood tests yet, still waiting to run our courses. I have four adult children.\[One with a seven year GBM brain tumour @pablosbrainjourney \] I am a VERY good hands on natural healer. My husband and myself will be teaching french polishing & antique furniture restoration courses when lockdown/Virus RESTRICTIONS lift. My husband had a pacemaker fitted in 2019 so we can't use the magnetico sleep pad. I wear a grounding band plugged into the power socket which helps me sleep. We've both been IF whilst eating strict LCHF since Jan 2018. Currently getting into 6am sunlight skin exposure whilst grounding on the grass bare foot, without getting frost bite, CT - icy cold showers, we don't have a bath.. yet. I am trying out exercising when I can 9-4pm(1-4pm) short sprinting, HIIT, weight lifting. I'm using progesterone cream, DHEA, D3 & K2, DHA, Lugol's 2% iodine for thyroid imbalance, anti-inflammatory's, kelp, dulse, more oily fish. I've been doing Dr Jack's leptin rx for 3.5 weeks now. Getting up near to 6am, eating fatty protein/lardo/grass fed butter eggs, bacon etc. Since adding the iodine and kelp supplements, dulse, my sleep has improved so much. I don't need to pee in the night. OMG I've been waking up, and staying awake sometimes for hours waiting to go back to sleep, pretty much every night since I started having babies 33years ago. SERIOUSLY! I think it could be something to do with thyroid.. and a crap vegetarian diet...What do you think? I want to wake up from the dopamine/blue light killer, doing whatever it takes to bring this about.

I have a few questions.....

1.  The clocks moving forward or back an hour. So do I continue to get up when the birdsong starts, regardless of manmade time OR do I get up, based on the 6am clock time?
2.  We have a big hill behind our house with some land and a stream running around half of it in Exeter. I go outside in the garden 5:30-6am. Does it matter if the hill hides the sun or is it enough to just get the beams, whether it's direct sun, clear sky with or without cloud coverage? How long a period of direct sun or partial sun with cloud etc? I've been walking up the hill to be level with the horizon 10 miles away. This is when the sky goes pinkish/red etc.

# Thread: The limitations of the Standard Model
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-limitations-of-the-standard-model.25658/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2021-03-30T17:55:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-limitations-of-the-standard-model.25658/post-296740>

The Standard Model can't explain: where the model predicts a particle called a beauty quark should decay into other particles called muons and electrons at about the same rate, it looks like it actually decays into electrons more often than muons.

New developments at CERN may have found a new force in Nature last week. This is exciting, because we already know the Standard Model doesn't tell the whole story about what's happening in the universe. It's very good at telling us about matter and energy. But it doesn't provide an account of the so-called dark matter and dark energy scientists believe must exist to explain the large-scale behavior of stars and galaxies.

The Standard Model is also tremendously difficult to reconcile with our best explanation of gravity, Einstein's theory of general relativity. The Standard Model is at best a step along the road to a complete theory of everything.
[https://www.theguardian.com/science/2021/mar/23/large-hadron-collider-scientists-particle-physics](https://www.theguardian.com/science/2021/mar/23/large-hadron-collider-scientists-particle-physics)

### John Schumacher — 2021-03-30T19:13:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-limitations-of-the-standard-model.25658/post-296747>

Thank you Dr. [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) - these theoretical models are simply not holding up to substantiated data. The only change has been the reforming of Einstein's theories. Perhaps, we may do better, if we "can", to step back from the canvas of evidence and ask better questions.

As a second “stroke of genius,” Albert Einstein invented the curvature of space through gravity because he could not explain how celestial bodies were kept in their orbit. Einstein himself must have taken it as a joke because he confessed to his friend Paul Ehrenfest: “_I’ve already done something wrong in the theory of gravity, which puts me at risk of being interned in a madhouse_.” 4 Only anyone who knows something about physics and geometry knows that **curvatures can only be observed on surfaces** and that light rays are influenced in their direction by diffraction due to optical effects.

Space, even "empty" space contains electromagnetic fields (EM), (Birkeland currents).
Celestial bodies, (asteroids), traveling thru space have their own EM fields, that are affected by existing EM fields.
When going thru Solar System smaller asteroids have a better chance of hitting Earth directly while larger asteroids may be deflected from the Earth (and at the best cause (electrical) scaring of Earth surface).

These "scientific" articles have always been very entertaining to me. The repeated belief system "quoting" previous statements, trying to substantiate the next discovery within the enlightenment of evolutionary presuppositions supporting the entrenched dogma that this is "the scientific method". Please read: "Cosmosapiens" by John Hands

What may we learn from our beloved dinosaurs?

-   The earth's gravitational force was exponentially less than it is today - [https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2015EGUGA..17..300S/abstract](https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2015EGUGA..17..300S/abstract)
-   The Infrared spectrum was much greater than it is now - [https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/feart.2020.00230/full](https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/feart.2020.00230/full)

I could go on and on with evidence, data points, etc. which are not calculated into the "evolutionary scientific method" of probabilities.
What is important for me is not to disprove this paradigm because you can't disprove a belief system.

My focus is to develop an understanding of what, how & why do Natural processes work in the way they do; and from this _knowledge_ speculate -> what are our human responsibilities and roles, and how are we to be played them out as "intelligent" creatures of Nature.

[https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.p...is-it-wrapped-in-our-human-environment.25380/](https://forum.jackkruse.com/index.php?threads/is-health-a-supplementation-of-multiple-interventions-or-is-it-wrapped-in-our-human-environment.25380/)

### Penny — 2021-04-01T07:38:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/professor-dolores-cahill-gets-into-the-pathogenic-priming-of-rna-vax-excellent.25665/post-296800>

Partial Transcript:

Then the real adverse events will happen, against whatever is the real mRNA in the vaccines, and when the person vaccinated comes across (this coronavirus) sometime later …. what happened in the animal studies, 20% or 50% or 100% of the animals died. Among people over 80, maybe about 2.5% will experience severe side effects, adverse events where people cannot work or live life normally.

Then with the 2nd vaccination it could be 1 in 10 or ten percent. For the over 80-year-olds, I would think that 80% of them would have life-limiting reactions or die when they come across the messenger RNA again.

For others (not elderly) it could be half of the people who could be severely harmed.

What it does is this gene therapy or medical device is setting up an autoimmune disease chronically.

It’s anaphylaxis in the first wave. It’s anaphylaxis +allergic reaction the 2nd wave. But the 3rd reaction occurs when you come across whatever the messenger RNA is against (virus), and now you have stimulated your immune system to have a low-grade autoimmune disease, not immunity to yourself per se because the mRNA is expressing a viral protein.

Now you’ve made yourself into a genetically modified organism, and so the immune system that is meant to push the viruses or bacteria out… now the autoimmune reaction is attacking your body low grade.

Now months later when you come across the virus that stimulates the immune system to get rid of the virus and when the immune system sees that you have viral proteins in your own cells and organs, then about a week later (the adaptive immune system kicks in, the mechanism that makes specific long-term memory antibodies against a pathogen) and you go into organ failure.

Because your immune system is killing your own organs. Those patients will present as sepsis initially. Then later you die of organ failure.

If you have one or two co-morbidities, the energy the immune system requires will make the older person very tired and exhausted and they don’t have the capacity to survive if you have underlying conditions.

Normally, because the mRNA is in every cell of their body, it’s almost unstoppable. It destroys the heart, or the spleen, or the lungs, or the liver because the mRNA is expressing the protein in every cell.

Just as a solution, what we urgently need, just as a repository, 1 in 100, or 1 in 200 vaccine vials injected, to be set aside, especially into the elderly in the care homes. They need to be stored in a biorepository of the vaccine vials randomly, so when the people start to die, we can actually see what is in this vaccine. We should be doing this now.

I am concerned that there are maybe multiple mRNAs in this vaccine, not just something for coronavirus.

We urgently need quality control to randomly require doctors to give 1 in 100 vaccine vials to a repository and someone like me could forensically analyze what’s in these vaccines.

So, when the elderly start dying, we will know. We should be knowing now what’s in them. It’s absolutely a dangerous gene therapy. Should not be given to the elderly,” emphasized professor Cahill.

# Thread: Hi to Everyone
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-to-everyone.25667/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### stuart buchanan — 2021-04-01T19:01:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hi-to-everyone.25667/post-296811>

Hi my name is Stuart, I'm 60yo, and I live by myself in a town close to Glasgow, Scotland. I was diagnosed with Parkinsons 5 years ago, and since then i have changed my lifestyle quite a lot. I am determined to do my best to overcome this illness. Right from the word go i have been looking into natural remedies. At the start, with the help of my Colombian girlfriend at the time, I came back from Colombia with 15kg of Mucuna Pruriens in my rucksack - this led to a couple of tense moments at airport security - added a bit of spice to life. (unfortunately there was no way to get the MP's tested and I only used so much in the end before being forced down the prescription drug route). However, approx 18 months ago, I started using a Root / Plant called Rhodiola, and have been successful in my attempt not to take any prescription meds.

I have engaged many professionals utilising different methodes to help my condition, and I have underwent many tests.

Going back about 3-4 months I saw an excellent Podcast from a guy called Luke Storey and I subsequently bought his package on EMF - something that was completely new to me. Included in the package as bonus items were two podcasts (I think) involving Dr Jack Kruse. I had never heard of him before. I immediately related to his style and intellect, and so I am here.

Looking forward to catching up with a few of you.

Best Wishes
Stuart

# Thread: My Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.25668/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 54
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DebraGM — 2021-04-02T01:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-optimal-journal.25668/post-296818>

I’m now 54 years old and I’ve continually gotten sicker for the last 4 years since 2017. I’ve gained 65 lbs, my energy is low, I had to give up coffee because GERD has set in, and I have anxiety which has affected my capacity to drive, to travel and to socialize, and I have sleep issues. I wear RA optics at night but I’m on my phone too much.

I’ve had previous bouts of health challenges. I gained and lost 80 lbs when I had my 2 children 1999-2003. I started getting ill when I was in graduate school for OT starting 1995. I had to dissect bodies and the formaldehyde bothered me AND I was a vegetarian 1995-99. I also received various vaccines to work in a hospital at that time. It was a time of high stress as well. I nursed both of my children for 2 years each, and lost all the weight and was thin and fit 2004 until I was diagnosed with Hashimoto's 2010. I was freezing, white as a ghost, couldn’t stand light and gained 30lbs. I believe this was triggered by having a mercury filling taken out without precautions. I instantly felt terribly ill. I had many tests, went to many doctors and spent a lot of money. I finally went to a holistic dentist who removed the rest of my mercury fillings and I started to feel better. But It wasn’t until I was given small amounts of hydrocortisone for adrenals that I felt like myself again.From 2013-2017 I was doing great as far as weight and energy. I was working as a health coach and personal trainer and I was a director of a holistic cancer organization where I did podcast interviews with numerous doctors and healers I was gluten/dairy/sugar free.

In 2017 I went through menopause, and I was taking care of my father with stage 4 prostate cancer, my mother with stage 2 laryngeal cancer, and 2 kids after divorce (one with Asperger’s syndrome). Stress was so high and I felt myself spiral down. Weight started increasing and energy decreasing. By 2018 I had gained 15 lbs and felt crappy. I had been self treating my Hashimoto's for years with 25 mcg T3 cytomel 2x a day, because Synthoid made me feel worse and no Dr would listen. My TSH was normal (about 1.0). I seemed to have a problem converting T4 to T3. Every year after that to the present I have continued to gain weight and continued to feel more claustrophobic and fearful. I went from a beautiful fit trainer to a fat introvert.

Some more pertinent information:

I was bottle fed.
Lived in Philadelphia until age 10.
Had vaginal yeast infections as young as age 5 and had bad hay fever until my 40s.
I developed asthma around 19 that required inhalers. I went on an anti inflammatory diet and my asthma seemed to go away. I get a bad respiratory illness every winter where I need steroids and inhaler, but other than that I haven’t needed an inhaler in over 15 years. This past winter I started with heartburn that got increasingly worse. I can’t eat large meals, chocolate, tomato sauce etc. I can’t lay down after I eat or bend over. I was on Prilosec for a month and weened off of it.
I stopped taking thyroid meds a year ago. It wasn’t helping and I wanted to see if my suppressed TSH would bounce back to 1.0. It did. T3 levels were low normal. I never took meds again.

I can’t take Vitamin D. It makes me sick. Sperti D light made me sick-aggressive, breathless, overstimulated. Taking B vitamins has a similar effect. I take Vitamin C, Zinc and Magnesium citrate and silver sometimes, but that’s it.

I was always the canary in the coal mine. I stayed away from toxic smells, GMOs, I don’t use a microwave. I use natural products and cleaners and eat organic home cooked food.
I moved 2 years ago (only about 3 min from my last townhouse in large development in a northern suburb an hour from Philadelphia. I now live in a development of only 13 homes. The electric lines are buried. Each home has an acre of land. Pa is a mandatory smart meter state. You can’t opt out. I did buy a faraday cage for the outside of it. But my head buzzes a lot...tinnitus. I installed all incandescent lights. My internet is hard wired and my WiFi is turned off at night.

I have the BRCA 2 gene. My paternal grandmother and all 5 of her sisters died from breast cancer, and my father from prostate cancer at 70. My father became a police officer in his late 40s, worked shift work and developed a love for casinos. I think that was his demise because otherwise he was always fit, no meds ever.

As a teen I had abdominal Shingles and Mononucleosis

I do not get mammograms. Although I did get one at 30 and until the age of 40. I get thermograms. So far they’ve been normal.

My vitamin D has been low for some time. I am going to get blood work soon. I’ll post results.

I have gone into anaphylactic shock twice. Once from HCG diet shot 2012 and once from doxycycline and porcine thyroid med I took at the same time. Very scary. I’m afraid to eat shellfish and peanuts. So scared of going through that again. I love seafood though. I want to be able to eat that again

I am totally in with Bitcoin and want to use my position to move to Florida ASAP. Jack I speak to you on Clubhouse often and I don’t miss a word you say or write. I hope to do a Skype consult soon.

### Jack Kruse — 2021-04-02T03:16:12+0300
